## Assisting Clients with Green Card Applications
Obtaining a Green Card can be an elaborate process. With so many steps along the way, it is important that you work with a legal professional you can trust to put you on the right path forward. We want you to choose a Tampa Green Card attorney from Brown Immigration Law.
At Brown Immigration Law , we provide detailed guidance for individuals, families, and corporations pursuing permanent residence. Located in Tampa, our immigration lawyers assist clients at every stage of the Green Card process, from eligibility review to final approval.

## Understanding Green Card Eligibility
Securing a Green Card is a significant step toward obtaining U.S. citizenship. There are three ways to seek a Green Card:
- Family-Based Green Cards: Available to close relatives of U.S. citizens and lawful permanent residents. This category includes spouses, children, parents, and, in some cases, siblings.
- Employment-Based Green Cards: These are offered to individuals with permanent employment opportunities in the U.S. Applicants may need an employer sponsor, and the category is based on profession and skills.
- Diversity Visa Lottery: An annual program for countries with low immigration rates to the U.S., offering an opportunity to apply for a Green Card.

## The Green Card Application Process
Our Tampa immigration attorneys assist clients throughout each step of the Green Card application process, keeping them informed and prepared from filing through interview. To meet the stringent requirements of the U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS), you must gather all necessary documentation, such as birth certificates, proof of employment, and legal status.
Our Tampa immigration attorneys assist you in navigating these steps, keeping you informed and prepared throughout. Brown Immigration Law excels at meticulous preparation, ensuring that all documentation and criteria are thoroughly addressed to ensure a smoother application process.
Are you seeking reliable legal support for your green card application? Contact us now to schedule your consultation and take the first step toward permanent residency!

- Community Involvement: Engaging with local communities can provide additional support and networking opportunities during your immigration journey.
Given Tampa’s strategic position and growing economic prospects, it attracts many immigrants seeking opportunities and better living standards. Many individuals begin their search for an immigration lawyer in Tampa after receiving a job offer, starting a family, or planning long-term residence in the area. ## FAQs About Green Cards

## Do I need a Green Card application lawyer in Tampa?
Legally, you do not need a Green Card application lawyer. However, immigration is challenging in America even under the best of circumstances. Applicants often face language barriers, complex paperwork, and stringent eligibility criteria. Misunderstanding the process or missing deadlines can complicate matters further. Errors or omissions can lead to application delays or denials. Many applicants choose to work with a green card lawyer to reduce the risk of delays, requests for evidence, or denials during the application process.

## Do I need a Tampa immigration lawyer to renew my Green Card?
Again, you never need to hire a lawyer. And it can be tempting, we know, to do it on your own and save a few dollars. However, if you need expedited renewal or have some unusual issues that could lead to a denial, we believe you’re better off working with an immigration attorney from the start. Brown Immigration Law has helped thousands of people on their path to permanent residency, and we can help you, too.

## How much time does it take to get a Green Card in Tampa?
It depends on the Green Card. Family-based residency can take months; investor-based residency can take years. And while federal procedures largely determine the process timeframe, local factors can also play a role. Working with a knowledgeable Tampa immigration lawyer can help expedite processes that fall under local jurisdiction. It also means having an advocate on your side to ensure your application doesn’t fall through the cracks and that you’re not subject to additional avoidable delays.

## Can people with Green Cards vote?
In the United States, Green Card holders do not automatically have the right to vote in federal or state elections. Non-citizens, including permanent legal residents who hold a Green Card, cannot vote in most elections. This can be frustrating for those who are working, dedicated members of the community. However, the right to vote is reserved specifically for U.S. citizens who meet other qualifications based on residency and age.
For those who wish to vote, working towards earning your U.C. citizenship may be worthwhile. You can also engage in civic events like public meetings and community organizations. Speak to a Tampa Green Card attorney about the path to citizenship for Green Card holders.

## Can a Green Card holder sponsor Green Cards for their parents?
As a Green Card holder, you are eligible to remain in the United States as long as you meet specific requirements. However, to petition to bring parents, such as your mother or father, into the country to live here as their own Green Card holders, you must be a U.S. citizen of at least 21 years of age.
In other words, if you are a Green Card holder, you cannot petition for your parents to live in the U.S. permanently. This is another excellent reason to begin working towards earning your U.S. citizenship. Once you do, you then gain the right to petition for your parents to live in the U.S. if they meet all other requirements. An immigration attorney can explain how the transition from permanent residence to citizenship affects family sponsorship rights.

## Can you transfer a license with a Green Card?​
If you have a driver’s license in another country, that usually means you have met the requirements for driving a vehicle safely. However, U.S. laws are a bit different. While you can use your foreign license for a short trip in the U.S., if you plan to remain longer, you can apply for an International Driving Permit from your home country prior to traveling, allowing you to drive in the U.S. once you arrive.
However, if you plan to live in the U.S., you need to follow the rules of the state in which you live to ensure that you meet foreign driver requirements. If you are a legal resident of the U.S. with a valid Green Card, you will be able to transfer your driver’s license.
Obtaining a driver’s license in the U.S. can be beneficial, especially if your old license has expired. To do so, you will need to provide valid data that shows your legal residency in the U.S., including identity verification documents and proof of legal status. You will have to pass a vision test, a written knowledge test, and a road test – like every citizen does – to obtain your license. There are also various application fees you may need to pay. A green card lawyer can also help confirm whether your immigration status documentation meets state licensing requirements.
Those who have an international professional license, such as a medical license, may be able to transfer their license to the U.S. as long as the local state board for their industry approves the education and training they received. This is often specific to the industry and your current professional license status.

## Where is the card number on a Green Card?
The Green Card number is on the bottom of the back of your card. It is in the first line in a string of digits of 90 characters in length. If your card is lost or damaged, an immigration lawyer from our team can assist with replacement applications.

## Can Green Card holders get Medicare or other federal benefits?
If you have been working in the U.S. for the necessary length of time. Just like U.S. residents, you must have contributed enough quarters of work (40 quarters or 10 years). This allows you to qualify for Original Medicare once you reach the necessary retirement age, as well as Railroad Retirement Benefits or Social Security Disability Insurance if you meet the qualifications. A Tampa immigration attorney can review eligibility rules for federal benefits based on your work history and status.

## Can undocumented immigrants get a Green Card?
In an ideal world, a person applies for and receives a Green Card prior to coming to the U.S. or through other approved methods. However, you may be able to get a Green Card if you are an undocumented immigrant and you meet specific requirements. Because this can be a complex process, it is often beneficial to work with a Tampa Green Card attorney to find out the ideal steps to citizenship you should take.

## Can you sit for a citizenship test with an expired Green Card?
If your Green Card expires, you may be able to request an extension that would allow you to apply for and sit for a U.S. citizenship test. You will need to have a valid form of identification to enter the facility to take your citizenship test. An immigration attorney can help you request temporary proof of status if your Green Card expires during the citizenship process.

## How much does a Tampa immigration lawyer cost for a Green Card?
The cost of hiring an immigration lawyer for a Green Card case in Tampa depends on the complexity of your situation and the specific legal services required. However, the investment can be invaluable in ensuring your application is thoroughly prepared, legally compliant, and submitted without unnecessary delays. Legal fees are typically determined by the services rendered, and most law firms will offer an initial consultation to discuss potential costs.
